html:
Exclusive to:

The Potato is a Vegetable Food Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together. Potatoes can be Farmed by planting Seeds with a moderate chance of growing them, by planting Fluffy Seeds, or by rummaging through a Junk Pile for a low chance at one.

When eaten raw, Potato restores 12.5 Hunger at the cost of 5 Sanity and 3 Health. It takes 10 days to spoil. It can be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes and is required for Creamy Potato Purée, Fancy Spiralled Tubers, Grim Galette, and Puffed Potato Soufflé. Feeding a Potato to a Bird in a Birdcage will produce 1 Fluffy Seeds.

Roasted Potato

Roasted Potato is obtained by cooking a Potato over a fire. It restores 20 health instead of costing 3 health, deducts no Sanity, and restores 12.5 more hunger than a raw Potato. It takes 6 days to spoil. It has the same Crock Pot uses as its raw variant.

It is Wolfgang's favorite food and consuming it will restore 15 more .

Usage

×2

Recipes

×2

Filler cannot be Twigs or Meats

×2

Filler cannot be Meats or Monster Food. One filler can be an additional Twigs.

×2

Filler cannot be Twigs or Meats

×2

The Gorge

Only during the Event: The Gorge
Potatoes are featured in The Gorge event in Don't Starve Together. They grow from Fluffy Seeds, which can be bought from Sammy for one Old Coin. A patch of four potatoes can be found already grown in the Swamp Pig village. A Potato can be made into a Roast Potato by being cooked at the Campfire next to the Swamp Pig Elder. Potatoes can be used in a variety of Cooking Recipes exclusive to The Gorge.

Recipes

×2

×2

×2

×2

Trivia

  • The Potato was originally exclusive to The Gorge event, but it was later introduced into the main game along with the Warly Character Update.
  • Winona's quote (I just think they're neat.) is a reference to The Simpsons.
  • Wolfgang's quote when examining a Roasted Potato (Is golden brown! Texture like sun!) is a reference to the song "Golden Brown" by The Stranglers.
  • Winona's quote (I've always been a meat and potatoes gal.) for a cooked potato references the common North-American saying which means 'just the basic necessities', referencing her character as the humble factory worker who doesn't need anything fancy.

Gallery

  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.

“The apples of the earth.”
–Wilson, when examining a Potato.
“We grew it in that gross muck.”
–Willow, when examining a Potato.
“Wolfgang's favorite veggie.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Potato.
“It has eyes, yet it never cries.”
–Wendy, when examining a Potato.
“I FEEL KINSHIP WITH THIS STRANGE ROOT”
–WX-78, when examining a Potato.
“Solanum tuberosum, a staple in some cultures.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Potato.
“I love good local produce.”
–Woodie, when examining a Potato.
“It is in the nightshade family, you know.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Potato.
“Vileness emerged fröm the earth.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Potato.
“Woah! A potato!”
–Webber, when examining a Potato.
“Ah, the mighty potato!”
–Warly, when examining a Potato.
“Thanks, dirt!”
–Wormwood, when examining a Potato.
“I just think they're neat.”
–Winona, when examining a Potato.
“Mortals like this in all its forms.”
–Wortox, when examining a Potato.
“Good dirt veggie.”
–Wurt, when examining a Potato.
“Should we mash them?”
–Walter, when examining a Potato.
“One of the more dependable vegetables.”
–Wanda, when examining a Potato.

“A successful temperature experiment.”
–Wilson, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“Cooking just means sticking stuff in fire.”
–Willow, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“Is golden brown! Texture like sun!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“Now its eyes will never open.”
–Wendy, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“YOU WILL BE DELICIOUS, STRANGE ROOT”
–WX-78, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“Roasted Solanum tuberosum.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“It cooked up real nice.”
–Woodie, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“The potato may be eaten now.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“It is öf nö use tö me.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“It's a cooked potato now.”
–Webber, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“Golden brown. Simplicity at its finest.”
–Warly, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“Dirt makes good belly stuff”
–Wormwood, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“I've always been a meat and potatoes gal.”
–Winona, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“A roasted mortal food.”
–Wortox, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“Mmm, hot potato!”
–Wurt, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“I love a campfire-roasted potato.”
–Walter, when examining a Roasted Potato.
“Potatoes are much better cooked, I'll admit.”
–Wanda, when examining a Roasted Potato.
